questions.question: Which tag defines a single cell of data in an HTML table?
questions.explanation: The <td> (table data) tag defines a standard data cell in a table. It must be placed inside a <tr> (table row) element.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the <thead>, <tbody>, and <tfoot> tags?
choices: They add borders to the table
They group rows into header, body, and footer sections for semantics and styling
They are required for the table to render
questions.explanation: These tags semantically group table rows into header, body, and footer sections. They enable separate styling, allow the body to scroll independently, and help screen readers understand table structure.

questions.question: Which attribute makes a cell span across multiple columns?
questions.explanation: The colspan attribute on a <td> or <th> element specifies how many columns the cell should span. For example, colspan="3" makes the cell stretch across three columns.

questions.question: What does the <th> tag represent compared to <td>?
choices: A header cell that is bold and centered by default
A cell with a thick border
A cell that cannot be edited
questions.explanation: The <th> (table header) tag defines a header cell. Browsers render it bold and centered by default. It also carries semantic meaning, helping screen readers identify column or row headers.

questions.question: The ___ tag is used to provide a title or description for an HTML table.
questions.explanation: The <caption> tag adds a visible title to a table. It must be the first child of the <table> element and helps users understand the table's purpose.

questions.question: The ___ attribute on a <th> element indicates whether it is a header for a row or a column.
questions.explanation: The scope attribute can be set to "col", "row", "colgroup", or "rowgroup" to clarify the header's relationship. It is important for accessibility, helping screen readers associate headers with data cells.
